object.label: init GPT component
OpenAI API call
repeat counter ++
object.Code: __repCounter++;

object.label: bot_said_wait
object.Keywords: one moment, hold on, one moment, me a sec, me a second, wait, please wait, a moment, moment please, one second, few seconds, be right with you, a sec, stand by, bear with me, stay on the line

object.label: process function output
clear called functions history and all system instructions
instruct the bot to say initial message

check if output is valid,
do confirmation logic
and add func to history
notify GPT about wrong function call
instruct the bot to say it again

mxCell.value:
__gptFunctionCall == "user_in_progress"
please better to call "transformFunctionName" to fix small differences in casing in the function name

NOTE FOR SWITCH bellow:
Warn: __resultArrived set on onNodeEnter

object.label: set output relation to "max loops"
object.Code: __outputRelation = __gptMaxLoopsLabel; /* it could be 'max loops' but this will work if somebody will change the label */

object.label: instruct the bot to make sure
log warning about users inactivity
object.Code: log_warn('No input from user! Conversation terminated!');

mxCell.value: <font style="font-size: 27px">protection to infinite loop when no user input</font>

object.label: enable VAD
disable VAD and wait until audio is playing
set output relation to "no input"
object.Code: __outputRelation = __gptNoInputLoopsLabel; /* it could be 'max loops' but this will work if somebody will change the label */

object.label: intent classifier
check intent classifier response
GPT request prepare
OpenAI API call
continue dialog based on objective/set result
object.OnEnter: if (__logPrompt) log_debug('DialogNode entry: Continue dialog based on objective/set result');
object.Title: continue dialog based on objective/set result

object.label: start by speech recognition
object.OnEnter: if (__logPrompt) log_debug('DialogNode entry: Start by speech recognition');
object.Title: start by speech recognition

mxCell.value: <font style="font-size: 27px">This logic determines whether the bot speaks or recognizes speech when it enters the component.</font>

object.label: postprocess result (HTML or Markdown

mxCell.value: <font style="font-size: 27px">Show output from RAG (contains HTML/Markdown) without XML escaping (chatbot only)</font>

object.label: counter reset
object.Code: __streamWaitCounter = 0;

object.label: new rec
evaluate what to say as wait message

mxCell.value: <font style="font-size: 27px">This logic will solve situation where bot will produce "one moment please" but not fire gpt function (we will force to call GPT again)</font>
<font style="font-size: 27px">initialize Dialog Node logic</font>
<font style="font-size: 27px">Control is dynamically transferred to this node using JavaScript if there has been an interruption—it must not be deleted!</font>
<font style="font-size: 27px">Main STT node</font>
<font style="font-size: 27px">This logic determines whether the bot calls LLM after speech recognition or asks "Can you repeat that?" (for example, when speech recognition confidence is low).</font>
<font style="font-size: 27px">This is confirmation message after each users input, currently not used (we do this dynamically - see bellow)</font>

VAD initialization,
classifier intent, and LLM request preparation
preventStreaming means that we don't need streaming and we just need to wait for a response (good for some chatbots where we dont want to stream output)

<font style="font-size: 27px">Probably the most complex logic - it decides whether the bot will say a confirmation message (short = So, long = Give me a sec), or play a keyboard sound, or try to call LLM again (if there was an error, or the response is not returned quickly enough).</font>
Here we wait for the intent classifier—each LLM DialogNode calls LLM twice after speech recognition (once for conducting dialogue and calling actions, and once as a pure intent classifier).
<font style="font-size: 27px">This is unlikely to happen; it is a backward compatibility issue and will be removed.</font>
<font style="font-size: 27px">We leave the LLM component by calling an action or recognizing an intent.</font>
<font style="font-size: 27px">This is what happens if the bot calls the gpt function (action) instead of generating speech.</font>
<font style="font-size: 27px">Say response from LLM</font>
<font style="font-size: 27px">Say response from LLM</font>
<font style="font-size: 27px">Here we decide whether to play the keyboard sound or skip it if it has already been played.</font>
The Dialog node Component is used to control part or all of the dialogue with the user.
